mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and elevated household savings rates creates a dual engine of economic growth and pent-up consumer demand, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of instances where both series trend upward concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a high inflation regime indicates supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to tighten policy aggressively, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines while CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the signal shows no regime-dependent directional flip in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage expenditures, which support corporate profit margins and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 800 thousand units signal robust construction sector demand and strong household balance sheets, which precedes broader economic expansion and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of months where HOUST surpasses 800.0, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising consumer price inflation and stagnant federal funds rates compresses real interest rates, reducing the cost of capital and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising unemployment during a late-cycle expansion confirms economic deceleration, prompting equity market repricing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where both INDPRO and UNRATE move in the specified directions, or the signal fails to flip direction when the macro regime shifts to early-cycle recovery.
